revision.text:
[[en:Amanawa Shinmei Shrine]][[fr:Amanawa Shinmei-jinja]][[ja:甘縄神明神社]][[ru:Храм Аманава Симмэй]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{Nihongo|Amanawa Shinmei Shrine|甘縄神明神社|Amanawa Shinmei Jinja|qq=}} | image = Amanawa1.jpg | caption = Stone stairs of Amanawa Shinmei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.31447|139.53704|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline|qq=}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name = Amanawa Shinmei Shrine |image = [[File:Amanawa-shinmei-jinja.jpeg|260px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|18|51.9|N|139|32|13.4|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kamakura City|Kamakura City]], [[Hase (Kamakura City)|Hase]] 1-12-1 |ISO = JP-14 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 18 |緯度秒 = 51.9 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 32 |経度秒 = 13.4 |deity = [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]] |神体 = |Rank = [[Modern shrine Ranking system#Village shrines|Former Village shrine]] |established = [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 3rd year (710) |main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternative name = Amanawa Shinmei-gū, Amanawa Shinmei-sha |札所等 = |festival= |map = Japan Kanagawa }} '''{{Nihongo|Amanawa Shinmei Shrine|甘縄神明神社|Amanawa Shinmei Jinja|qq=}}''' was founded in 710 and is the oldest {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}. It is dedicated to the sun goddess [[Amaterasu]]. According to the ancient document ''History of Amanawa-ji Shinmei-gū'' kept by the shrine, the founder of the shrine is famous priest {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}}; a powerful and rich man named Tokitada Someya supported the construction. The shrine consists of two structures: the [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] or oratory in the front and the ''[[honden]]'' or sanctum at the back. Both the oratory and the sanctum have hornlike crossing planks called {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|en|Chigi (architecture)|qid=Q262282|lt=''chigi''|1=Chigi (architecture)}} rising above the roof at both ends. Also five short logs called ''{{ill|Katsuogi|en|Katsuogi|qid=Q13479730|lt=Katsuogi|1=Katsuogi}}'' are placed at a right angle along the ridge of the roof. This is typical of a [[Shinto]] architectural style called [[Shinmei-zukuri]]. As the old structures were destroyed by the [[:en:1923 Great Kantō earthquake|1923 Great Kantō earthquake]], they were rebuilt in 1936. The last reconstruction was completed in 1998. The shrine is 10 minutes by walk from {{ill|Hase Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hase Station (Kanagawa)|qid=Q858830|lt=Hase Station|1=Hase Station (Kanagawa)}} and 20 minutes by walk from {{ill|Kamakura Station|en|Kamakura Station|qid=Q932895|lt=Kamakura Station|1=Kamakura Station}}. ==Overview== {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.314442|139.537068|qq=}}|caption=Amanawa Shinmei Shrine|width=256|qq=}} '''Amanawa Shinmei Shrine''' (あまなわしんめいじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hase|en|Hase|qid=Q700566|lt=Hase|1=Hase}}, [[:en:Kamakura City|Kamakura City]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. The former shrine Ranking was that of a [[Modern shrine Ranking system#Village shrines|village shrine]]. It is considered the oldest shrine in Kamakura City[[PHP Shinsho]] "Walking through 122 Temples and Shrines in Kamakura", p.195. It was formerly known as '''Amanawa Shinmei-gū''' or '''Amanawa Shinmei-sha''', but was renamed in {{ill|1932|en|1932|qid=Q18743|lt=1932|1=1932}} (Showa 7)[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]] "Dictionary of Old Shrines and Temples in Kamakura", p.116. == Deities == ; Main deity :* [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]] ; Enshrined deities :* [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] :* {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} :* [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} == Overview == According to the shrine's brief history, it is said to have been founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 3rd year (710), and built by the local clan leader [[Someya Tokitada]]. {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} is said to have been born after his father [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed at this shrine. Yoshiie rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|1081|en|1081|qid=Q19450|lt=1081|1=1081}} (Eihō 1). For these reasons, it is said to have been deeply revered by the [[Minamoto clan|Genji shogunate family]]. There is also a monument of "Adachi Morinaga Residence Site" near the shrine, indicating that the residence of {{ill|Adachi Morinaga|en|Adachi Morinaga|qid=Q2823877|lt=Adachi Morinaga|1=Adachi Morinaga}}, a close aide of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], was located here. However, recent theories suggest that it was located in [[Onarimachi (Kamakura City)|Onarimachi]] at the [[Imajōnishi Site]]. There used to be a temple called '''Amanawa-in''' as well, but it was abolished due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. In {{ill|1887|en|1887|qid=Q7826|lt=1887|1=1887}} (Meiji 20), Gosha Myojin, the guardian shrine of [[Hasedera Temple (Kamakura City)|Hasedera Temple]], was enshrined here. The current main hall and worship hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Showa 12). == Access == * About a 6-minute walk from {{ill|Hase Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hase Station (Kanagawa)|qid=Q858830|lt=Hase Station|1=Hase Station (Kanagawa)}} on the {{ill|Enoshima Electric Railway|en|Enoshima Electric Railway|qid=Q250760|lt=Enoden Line|1=Enoshima Electric Railway}} * No parking available == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == {{commonscat|Amanawa Shinmei Jinja Shrine|qq=}} {{osm box|n|1771099746|qq=}} * "Walking through 122 Temples and Shrines in Kamakura", [[PHP Shinsho]], ISBN 9784569810355 * "Dictionary of Old Shrines and Temples in Kamakura", [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], ISBN 9784642080606 * "Small Dictionary of Kamakura Shrines", [[Kamakura Shunjūsha]], ISBN 9784774002057 * "New Edition Kamakura Tourism Culture Test Official Textbook", Kamakura Chamber of Commerce, ISBN 9784774005386 * "Kamakura Children's Local History (13th Edition)", Kamakura City Board of Education * "Brief History of Amanawa Shinmei-gū", published by the shrine == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}} * [[List of shrines in Kamakura City]] == External Links == * [https://www.trip-kamakura.com/place/amanawa.html Amanawa Shinmei-gū] - Kamakura City Tourist Association * [https://hasenowa.com/spot/144/ Amanawa Shinmei-gū] - Kamakura Hasenowa {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}} ==Gallery== Amanawa-2.jpg|The [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] Amanawa-3.jpg|The [[honden]] Amanawa4.jpg|Stairs between the haiden and the honden Amanawa6.jpg|A [[Setsumatsusha|sessha]] at the shrine ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20120324100152/http://www.kamakurainfo.net/modules/myalbum/viewcat.php?cid=110 Kamakura Info] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110514105804/http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~QM9T-KNDU/amanawa.htm A New Guide to Kamakura] {{Shinto shrine|qq=}} {{Sun temples|qq=}} {{Shinmei shrines|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|甘縄神明神社|version=104045451|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Amanawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[ja:甘縄神明神社]] {{Commons category|Amanawa Shinmei Jinja Shrine}} [[fr:Amanawa Shinmei-jinja]] [[ru:Храм Аманава Симмэй]] {{Commons category|Amanawa Shinmei Jinja Shrine}} [[fr:Amanawa Shinmei-jinja]] [[ru:Храм Аманава Симмэй]] {{wikidata link|Q2841005}} [[Category:安達盛長]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kamakura]]
